sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46
sources.population: People presenting with cough; age limits are not stated in the applicability section.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth cough documentation; only partial for a general in-person workflow and for chronic-cough investigation pathways.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, Dubai purpose, licensed-provider applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Clinical History
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

exact_sections.heading: 8. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected blood tests, chest radiography, peak-flow diary, pulmonary function testing, sputum testing, ECG, and specialist-led advanced imaging.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led emergency, family-medicine, and pulmonary referral according to the documented cough context.
